definition: The usual weather conditions in an area
example: Climate change is a global issue.

definition: Harmful substances released into the environment
example: Air pollution is a problem in big cities.

definition: To convert waste into reusable material
example: We should recycle plastic and paper.

definition: A resource that can be replaced naturally
example: Solar power is a renewable energy source.

definition: All living things in an area and how they interact
example: The rainforest has a complex ecosystem.

definition: The release of gas or radiation into the air
example: Carbon emissions must be reduced.

definition: Able to continue without harming the environment
example: We need sustainable farming practices.

definition: The variety of plant and animal life in an area
example: Biodiversity is threatened by deforestation.

definition: Remains of ancient animals or plants in rock
example: Fossil fuels take millions of years to form.

definition: The protection of natural resources
example: Conservation efforts have saved many species.

definition: The clearing of trees in a large area
example: Deforestation destroys animal habitats.

definition: A long period without rain
example: The drought killed many crops.

definition: Unwanted materials thrown away
example: We should reduce food waste.

definition: A chemical element found in all living things
example: We need to reduce our carbon footprint.

definition: At risk of extinction
example: Pandas are an endangered species.

definition: Relating to the whole world
example: Global warming affects everyone.

definition: Grown without artificial chemicals
example: I prefer organic vegetables.

definition: The natural home of an animal or plant
example: Wetlands are an important habitat for birds.

definition: A glass building for growing plants; also relates to warming gases
example: Greenhouse gases trap heat in the atmosphere.

definition: The dying out of a species
example: Many animals face extinction.
